characters.name: Ally Williams
characters.description: Ally WilliamsFirst appearance"Desking"Last appearance"Wrong Delivery"Portrayed byAmbrit MillhouseCharacter informationFull nameAlly Williams[1]Nickname(s)Miss WilliamsOccupationTeacherGenderFemaleAffiliationAbbott ElementaryAlly Williamsalso known asMiss Williams, is a recurring character onAbbott Elementary. She is one of the few veteran teachers at Abbott. She is portrayed by Ambrit Millhouse. InDesking, Ally was sitting in the breakroom when she overheard the other teachers talking of finding shoe prints on their desks. She chimed in saying that she too saw shoe prints on her classroom desks. When Janine reported the desking situation to the district, the school was instructed to move all desks to the gymnasium. Janine saw Ally moving her desks towards the gym and Ally informed her of the district's instructions. Ally further expressed that whoever thought that the district would help was an idiot.

characters.name: Amber Williams
characters.description: Amber WilliamsFirst appearance"Light Bulb"Last appearance"Teacher Conference"Portrayed byNaté JonesCharacter informationFull nameAmber Williams[1]GenderFemaleFamilyJoel Williams(son)Carolina Williams(daughter)[2]RelationshipsRashon(boyfriend)[3]Gregory Eddie(ex-boyfriend)[4]Amber Williamsis a recurring character inAbbott Elementary. She is the ex-girlfriend ofGregory Eddie. She is also the mother of Joel, a former student of Gregory,[1]and Carolina, Janine's student.[2] She is portrayed by Naté Jones. InLight Bulb, Amber was dropping off her son, Joel, to school an hour late.Avahad noticed that this had happened six times in the last two weeks, so she askedGregoryto do something about it. Gregory was not too keen on confronting a student's parent since he was a substitute teacher. He asked advice from Barbara about his predicament, soBarbaraadvised him to eat lunch with her at a nearby nail salon. When Gregory arrived at the nail salon, Barbara revealed that she knew the Williams family and that Amber went to the nail salon every two weeks, same as her. After some coaxing by Barbara, Gregory eventually talked to Amber. Amber was bringing her son late because her shift at her job didn't start until 9:30am, so it was easier for her to drop him off at around 9:00am and go straight to work. After Gregory explained how her son was falling behind on the lessons, Amber promised to get him to class on time.

characters.name: Ashley Garcia
characters.description: Ashley GarciaFirst appearance"Story Samurai"Last appearance"Aide"Portrayed byKeyla Monterroso MejiaCharacter informationFull nameAshley Garcia[1]OriginFrankford, Philadelphia[2]OccupationTeacher's aideGenderFemaleAshley Garciais a recurring character in the second season ofAbbott Elementary. She is a teacher's aide sent to helpMelissa Schemmenti. She is portrayed byKeyla Monterroso Mejia. Ashley enters the scene at the very end ofStory Samurai, arriving as relief from the district whenMelissafinally acceptsAva's offer of an aide for her combined 2nd/3rd grade class. She makes her grand entrance with a boombox (that she forgets to turn on), dancing and singing to her own rendition of Jennifer Lopez's "Waiting For Tonight": "Waiting for Ashley, ohh-ohh-ohh, I'll be the aide of your dreams." As Ashley shimmies back out of Ava's office, Melissa's face suggests this might not be the relief she hoped for, while the Story Samurai fall over themselves in the hallway.

characters.name: Ava Coleman
characters.description: Ava ColemanSeason 5Season 4Season 3Season 2Season 1First appearance"Pilot" (December 7, 2021)Last appearanceTBAPortrayed byJanelle JamesCharacter informationFull nameAva Eva ColemanAliasesMC Gilette(former)[3]Nickname(s)Saint Ava(by Barbara)[4]HBIC(by herself)[5]Baby(by O’Shon)OccupationPrincipal atAbbott ElementaryGenderFemaleFamilyLorraine(grandmother)[1]RelativesFrank Coleman(estranged father)Significant otherAndre "Iggy" Iguodala(ex-boyfriend)[6]O'Shon(boyfriend; in love)RelationshipsJanine Teagues(friend/co-worker)Barbara Howard(friend/co-worker)Melissa Schemmenti(friend/co-worker)Jacob Hill(co-worker)Gregory Eddie(co-worker)Mr. Johnson(co-worker)AffiliationAbbott ElementaryAbbott Steppers[1]Grand Canyon University(alumnus)Ava Coleman/GalleryAva Eva Coleman[7]is a main character in ABC'S hit mockumentaryAbbott Elementary. Ava is Abbott Elementary's inept principal despite displaying a high level of incompetence. Ava obtained her job as principal after blackmailing the superintendent. She harbored an attraction towardsGregory. HBIC(by herself)[5]Baby(by O’Shon) She is portrayed by American comedianJanelle James.

characters.name: Avi
characters.description: AviFirst appearancePanelLast appearancePartyPortrayed byKaran SoniCharacter informationFull nameAnandOccupationParamedicGenderMaleSignificant otherJacob Hill(ex-boyfriend)RelationshipsCaptain Robinson(boss)Olivia(co-worker/friend)StatusAliveAnand[1], colloquially referred to asAvi, is a reoccurring character in the third season ofAbbot Elementary. He works as a Paramedic at the local Philadelphia fire station. He is portrayed by Karan Soni. InPanel, Avi is working alongside Captain Robinson during the CPR training session for the Abbott teachers. During the session, it is revealed that him and Jacob hooked up on the roof of Melissa's house. When questioned by Melissa why he didn't tell her, Jacob states that the he felt it was just casual. Avi is disappointed thinking that Jacob was just being coy by ignoring his texts. Despite all this, Avi allures Jacob into pursuing him.

characters.name: Ayesha Teagues
characters.description: Ayesha TeaguesFirst appearance"Valentine's Day"Last appearance"Teacher Appreciation"Portrayed byAyo EdebiriCharacter informationFull nameAyesha TeaguesBornFebruary 14[1]Philadelphia, PennsylvaniaGenderFemaleFamilyJanine Teagues(half-sister)Vanetta Teagues(mother)Unnamed(father)Unnamed(step-mother)Ayesha Teaguesis a recurring character inAbbott Elementary. She is the sister ofJanine Teagues. She currently resides in Denver, Colorado[2]after moving there to get away from their mother. She is portrayed byAyo Edebiri. Not much is seen of Ayesha during the series. The people who meet her, such asJacoband Erika, find her cool and interesting. Due to her relationship with their mother, Ayesha always tends to only look out for herself. She sees herself as a screw-up because of how she compares to Janine, who she says is perfect.

characters.name: Courtney Pierce
characters.description: Courtney PierceFirst appearance"Student Transfer"Last appearance"Science Fair"Portrayed byLela HoffmeisterCharacter informationFull nameCourtney Pierce[1]GenderFemaleFamilyPatrick Pierce(father)[1]Courtney Pierceis a recurring character inAbbott Elementary. She was transferred toJanine Teagues' class until she was bumped up to third grade in season one. She is portrayed by Lela Hoffmeister. InStudent Transfer, Courtney transfers to Janine's class. When Janine heads toMelissa Schemmenti's classroom to pick up Courtney, Janine brags about being better than Melissa. While in Janine's class, she first tells the class her name is Janine Teagues. Then, when the class does the pledge of allegiance, the class replaces some words in the pledge with her name. It is later revealed that Courtney was transferred because Melissa caught onto her tricks, Melissa says in Courtney's File, "She could make a good cult leader someday." Janine tries to talk with Courtney, she offers to do a puzzle with her. This later backfires, as she writes **** puzzles with permanent marker on the chalkboard, they go into Melissa's classroom whileMr. Johnsoncleans the words off the board. She does someone's homework for them for money while waiting. When talking withAva Coleman, Courtney gets moved up to the 3rd Grade.

characters.name: Delisha Sloss
characters.description: Delisha SlossFirst appearance"Ava vs. Superintendent"Last appearance"Breakup"Portrayed byShirley JordanCharacter informationFull nameDelisha SlossOccupationSchool Board MemberGenderFemaleAffiliationPhiladelphia School Board Council[1]Delisha Slossis a recurring character inAbbott Elementary. She is a board member of the Philadelphia School Board Council. She also goes to the same church asBarbara Howard, where she is on its finance committee. She is portrayed by Shirley Jordan. InAva vs. Superintendent, Barbara and the other Abbott teachers were thinking of ways to help Ava in securing budget for the school. Knowing Ava's ineptitude, Melissa and Barbara agreed that there was no way that thesuperintendentwould approve their request for discretionary funds. There was a way, however to secure the Advancement Grant the the school board awards annually. Melissa suggested that they do a little arm-twisting to get the Grant. Melissa found a connection at the school board through Delisha Sloss who goes to the same church as Barbara. According to Melissa's guy, Delisha's on the finance committee at church and Barbara identified her as one of the shadier members of their church. Since Delisha has been skimming cash off the church's funds for years, Melissa suggested that they shake her down to ensure that they get the grant. Barbara hesitated initially.

characters.name: Denzel Collins
characters.description: Denzel CollinsFirst appearance"Open House"Last appearance"Ava vs. Superintendent"Portrayed byReggie HayesCharacter informationFull nameDenzel Collins[1]OccupationSuperintendent(former)GenderMaleRelationshipsUnnamed Deaconess Wife[2]Unnamed Ex-Wife[2]DenzelCollinsis a recurring character in thefirst seasonofAbbott Elementary. He was the superintendent of the Philadelphia school district which contains Abbott and appointedAvaprincipal of the school after she blackmailed him. InSeason 3, he was replaced as superintendent by someone named John Reynolds. He is portrayed by Reggie Hayes, who was nominated for the Black Reel Television Award for Outstanding Guest Actor, Comedy Series for his performance. Prior to the beginning of the series Collins appointedAvaas the principal of Abbott Elementary after she caught him cheating on his wife with the deaconess of his church and she blackmailed him into giving her the job to keep his secret hidden. In previous years he allocated Abbott generous funds as part of this blackmail.[3]

characters.name: Gary
characters.description: GaryFirst appearance"Gifted Program"Last appearance"Career Day"Portrayed byBruno AmatoCharacter informationAliasesVending Machine GuyOccupationVending Machine Operator[1]GenderMaleSignificant otherMelissa Schemmenti(Ex-girlfriend)Lucy(wife)Affiliationbpt Vending[1]Garyalso known as theVending Machine Guy, is a guest character inAbbott Elementary. He operates the vending machines inside Abbott Elementary. He is also the ex boyfriend ofMelissa Schemmenti. He is portrayed by Bruno Amato. InGifted Program, Gary was in the Abbott Elementary teachers' lounge like any Thursday to refill the vending machines. He and Melissa flirted with each other and Barbara noticed the chemistry between the two. She encouraged Melissa to ask Gary out. However, when Gary told Melissa of his plans for their date, Melissa got cold feet and immediately cancelled. After Melissa dealt with her fears of dating again, she asked Gary out once more and the two began dating soon after.

characters.name: Gerald Howard
characters.description: GeraldFirst appearance"Gifted Program"Last appearance"Mother's Day"Portrayed byRichard BrooksCharacter informationFull nameGerald HowardGenderMaleFamilyBarbara Howard(wife)Taylor Howard(daughter)Gina Howard(daughter)Zara(granddaughter; via Taylor)Gerald Howardis a guest character in thefirst seasonofAbbott Elementary. He is the husband ofBarbara Howard. He is portrayed byRichard Brooks. InGifted Program, Gerald went to Abbott Elementary to bring Barbara and Melissa their lunch. Melissa mentioned that Gerald was the bar for men to have a relationship with. Barbara told Melissa that Gerald used to be a player and a flirt but sometimes you had to take a leap of faith.

He is an awkward but supportive History teacher at Abbott. He is called by his students, young teens, as "Mr. C" because he's corny but also because they like him and is a good teacher - one of his students even wanted to become a teacher because of it.
